heading: Safe-to-Report policy for Coast Guard
label: 2535 Safe-to-Report policy for Coast Guard
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025, referred to in subsec. (a), is the date of enactment of div. G of , which was approved .Pub. L. 119–60Dec. 18, 2025

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025, the Commandant shall, in consultation with the Secretaries of the military departments, establish and maintain a detailed and publicly available safe-to-report policy described in subsection (b) that applies with respect to all members of the Coast Guard (including members of the reserve and auxiliary components of the Coast Guard), cadets at the Coast Guard Academy, and any other individual undergoing training at an accession point of the Coast Guard.
prescribes the handling of minor collateral misconduct, involving a member of the Coast Guard who is the alleged victim or reporting witness of a sexual assault; and

children.label: A
children.text: to whom the victim makes the allegation or who receives the victim’s report of sexual assault; or

children.label: B
children.text: whether the report, investigation, or prosecution is handled by military or civilian authorities.

children.heading: .—Mitigating and Aggravating Circumstances
children.label: c .—Mitigating and Aggravating Circumstances
children.text: In issuing the policy under subsection (a), the Commandant shall specify mitigating circumstances that decrease the gravity of minor collateral misconduct or the impact of such misconduct on good order and discipline and aggravating circumstances that increase the gravity of minor collateral misconduct or the impact of such misconduct on good order and discipline for purposes of the safe-to-report policy.

children.heading: .—Tracking of Collateral Misconduct Incidents
children.label: d .—Tracking of Collateral Misconduct Incidents
children.text: In conjunction with the issuance of the policy under subsection (a), the Commandant shall develop and implement a process to anonymously track incidents of minor collateral misconduct that are subject to the safe-to-report policy.
is committed close in time to or during a sexual assault and directly related to the incident that formed the basis of the allegation of sexual assault allegation;
is discovered as a direct result of the report of sexual assault or the ensuing investigation into such sexual assault; and

does not involve aggravating circumstances (as specified in the policy issued under subsection (a)) that increase the gravity of the minor misconduct or the impact of such misconduct on good order and discipline.
